title: Physiology of long pranayamic breathing: neural respiratory elements may provide a mechanism that explains how slow deep breathing shifts the autonomic nervous system
headline: Physiology of long pranayamic breathing: neural respiratory elements may provide a mechanism that explains how
summary: A mechanistic/observational study of pranayama (yogic breathing) examined brain & mechanisms. Both inhibitory impulses and hyperpolarization current are known to synchronize neural elements leading to the modulation of the nervous system and decreased metabolic activity indicative of the parasympathetic state.
keyFindings: Both inhibitory impulses and hyperpolarization current are known to synchronize neural elements leading to the modulation of the nervous system and decreased metabolic activity indicative of the parasympathetic state.
limitations: Observational/mechanistic design cannot establish that the practice caused the observed differences.
The practice studied (pranayama (yogic breathing)) is not the Miracle of Mind practice; findings do not transfer directly to MoM.
simplifiedTitle: Physiology of long pranayamic breathing: neural respiratory elements may provide a mechanism that explains how
